name: Emma
concern_text: Parents want a home plan for communication, motor skills, and daily routines.

asked.question: talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
says about 50 words
say two or more words toghether with one action word like doggie run
strings items together like large beads or macaroni
puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket
uses hands to twist things like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids
takes some clothes off by himself like loose pants or an open jacket
calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off
notices other children and joins them to play
plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
shows you what she can do by saying look at me
draws a circle when you show her how
avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her
uses things to pretend like feeding a block to a doll as if it were food
shows simple problem solving skills like standing on a small stool to reach something

speech_language.title: Picture-supported requesting
speech_language.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Speech / Language around 24-30 months.
speech_language.instructions: Use two preferred items and prompt the child to point, sign, or say what they want.
speech_language.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

speech_language.title: Expand their communication
speech_language.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Speech / Language around 24-30 months.
speech_language.instructions: Repeat the child's word and add one more word during play or snacks.
speech_language.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

speech_language.title: WH-question picture practice
speech_language.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Speech / Language around 24-30 months.
speech_language.instructions: Use picture cards to practice who, what, or where questions.
speech_language.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

speech_language.title: Routine-based language
speech_language.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Speech / Language around 24-30 months.
speech_language.instructions: Practice short phrases during snack, bath, or dressing routines.
speech_language.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

speech_language.title: Choice making with pause time
speech_language.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Speech / Language around 24-30 months.
speech_language.instructions: Offer a choice and wait 5 seconds before helping.
speech_language.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

motor.title: Supported obstacle path
motor.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Motor around 30-36 months.
motor.instructions: Create a short safe path for stepping, reaching, and balance.
motor.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

motor.title: Sit-to-stand repetitions
motor.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Motor around 30-36 months.
motor.instructions: Practice rising from a chair or cushion during play.
motor.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

motor.title: Reach and place game
motor.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Motor around 30-36 months.
motor.instructions: Place toys at different heights to practice reaching and controlled movement.
motor.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

motor.title: Ball roll and stop
motor.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Motor around 30-36 months.
motor.instructions: Roll a ball back and forth and add stop/go cues.
motor.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

motor.title: Core and balance play
motor.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Motor around 30-36 months.
motor.instructions: Use floor play positions that encourage trunk control and balance.
motor.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

social_communication.title: Turn-taking with favorite toy
social_communication.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Social / Communication around 30-36 months.
social_communication.instructions: Use a favorite toy and practice clear my-turn/your-turn routines.
social_communication.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

social_communication.title: Shared attention show-and-share
social_communication.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Social / Communication around 30-36 months.
social_communication.instructions: Encourage the child to show an object and look back to the caregiver.
social_communication.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

social_communication.title: Pretend play routine
social_communication.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Social / Communication around 30-36 months.
social_communication.instructions: Model a simple pretend routine like feeding a doll or stuffed animal.
social_communication.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

social_communication.title: Name-response game
social_communication.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Social / Communication around 30-36 months.
social_communication.instructions: Use playful name-calling and immediate reinforcement when the child responds.
social_communication.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

social_communication.title: Simple peer-style game
social_communication.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Social / Communication around 30-36 months.
social_communication.instructions: Play a short, rule-based game with waiting and imitation.
social_communication.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Two-step helper task
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Adaptive / Cognitive around 24-30 months.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Practice a short daily routine with two simple steps.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Visual cleanup routine
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Adaptive / Cognitive around 24-30 months.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use pictures or gestures for cleanup or dressing steps.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Matching and sorting
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Adaptive / Cognitive around 24-30 months.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Sort familiar objects by category, color, or function.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Mini task board
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Adaptive / Cognitive around 24-30 months.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use a tiny 2-3 step board for snack, dressing, or play setup.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Cause-and-effect problem solving
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Matches a child with chronological age 36 months and estimated Adaptive / Cognitive around 24-30 months.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use simple toys or household tasks that reward trial and error.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Reduce language demands, simplify the routine, and add modeling or support as needed.

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 24-30 months. Evidence: 36m: with_help to 'talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess'; 36m: with_help to 'ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy'; 30m: sometimes to 'says about 50 words'; 30m: sometimes to 'say two or more words toghether with one action word like doggie run'
Motor estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 36m: sometimes to 'strings items together like large beads or macaroni'; 36m: sometimes to 'puts on some clothes by himself like loose pants or a jacket'; 30m: yes to 'uses hands to twist things like turning doorknobs or unscrewing lids'; 30m: yes to 'takes some clothes off by himself like loose pants or an open jacket'
Social / Communication estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 36m: sometimes to 'calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off'; 36m: sometimes to 'notices other children and joins them to play'; 30m: yes to 'plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them'; 30m: yes to 'shows you what she can do by saying look at me'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 24-30 months. Evidence: 36m: with_help to 'draws a circle when you show her how'; 36m: with_help to 'avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her'; 30m: sometimes to 'uses things to pretend like feeding a block to a doll as if it were food'; 30m: sometimes to 'shows simple problem solving skills like standing on a small stool to reach something'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.

name: Noah
concern_text: Parents report limited back-and-forth play, speech delay, and rigid routines.

asked.question: tells a storys she heard or made up with at least two events for example cat was stuck in a tree and firefighter saved it
answers simple questions about a book or a story after you read or tell it to her
says sentences with four or more words
says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme
talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
buttons some buttons
hops on one foot
follows rules or takesake turns when playing games with other  children
sings dances or acts for you
pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog
asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex
calms down within 10 minutes after you leave her like at childcare drop off
notices other children and joins them to play
plays next to other children and sometimes plays with them
shows you what she can do by saying look at me
counts to 10
names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them
names a few colors or items
tell what comes next in a well known story
draws a circle when you show her how
avoids touching hot objects like the stove when you warn her

profile_text_for_prompt:
Speech / Language estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 60m: no to 'tells a storys she heard or made up with at least two events for example cat was stuck in a tree and firefighter saved it'; 60m: no to 'answers simple questions about a book or a story after you read or tell it to her'; 48m: with_help to 'says sentences with four or more words'; 48m: with_help to 'says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme'
Motor estimated around 60-60 months. Evidence: 60m: yes to 'buttons some buttons'; 60m: yes to 'hops on one foot'
Social / Communication estimated around 30-36 months. Evidence: 60m: no to 'follows rules or takesake turns when playing games with other  children'; 60m: no to 'sings dances or acts for you'; 48m: no to 'pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog'; 48m: no to 'asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex'
Adaptive / Cognitive estimated around 36-48 months. Evidence: 60m: no to 'counts to 10'; 60m: no to 'names some numbers between 1 and 5 when you point to them'; 48m: sometimes to 'names a few colors or items'; 48m: sometimes to 'tell what comes next in a well known story'
note: CDC table only goes to 60 months, so children older than 60 months start from the 60-month band as an anchor.
